retail.name: Retail Purchase
retail.description: Standard daily consumer transaction (e.g., grocery shopping or restaurant bill). Focuses on low ticket size and high merchant volume.

card_rail.name: Card Rail (Visa/Mastercard)
card_rail.counterparty_risk: High (Chargebacks possible up to 90-120 days, high fraud risk, rolling reserves required for merchants)
card_rail.governance: Centralized under Card Schemes & Clearing Banks

on_chain_rail.name: On-Chain (Bitcoin)
on_chain_rail.counterparty_risk: Zero (Cryptographically secured, irreversible settlement, no chargebacks or funds freezing)
on_chain_rail.governance: Decentralized (Consensus-based protocol, miners, and node operators)

b2b.name: B2B Wholesale Payment
b2b.description: Corporate settlement for raw materials or services. Characterized by very high transaction volume values and strict reconciliation requirements.

card_rail.name: Card Rail (Commercial Card)
card_rail.counterparty_risk: Medium (Lower fraud rates than retail, but high dispute value risk)

on_chain_rail.name: On-Chain (Bitcoin)
on_chain_rail.counterparty_risk: Zero (Irreversible cryptographic settlement, proof of payment verified on public ledger)

cross_border.name: Cross-Border Remittance
cross_border.description: International funds transfer crossing regulatory jurisdictions, requiring currency conversion and correspondent banking routing.

card_rail.name: Card Rail / SWIFT / Correspondent
card_rail.counterparty_risk: High (Multi-jurisdictional compliance, routing delays, FX slippage, clearing house risk)
card_rail.governance: Multi-bank clearing systems, SWIFT, and central bank rails (Fedwire, TARGET2)

on_chain_rail.name: On-Chain (Bitcoin)
on_chain_rail.counterparty_risk: Zero (Border-free network, direct wallet-to-wallet settlement, zero clearing risk)

micropayment.name: Micropayment / Pay-Per-Article
micropayment.description: Sub-dollar transaction for digital content, API calls, or pay-per-use services, where traditional fees make card rails cost-prohibitive.

card_rail.name: Card Rail (Visa/Mastercard)
card_rail.counterparty_risk: Extremely High (Micropayments are often unprofitable if chargebacks or dispute fees of $15+ occur)

on_chain_rail.name: On-Chain (Bitcoin)
on_chain_rail.counterparty_risk: Zero (Irreversible micro-settlement on-chain, though Lightning is recommended for lower sizes)

fed_study_context.title: Federal Reserve Payments Study & BIS CPMI Data
fed_study_context.description: Baseline stats from the Fed Payments Study indicate the average card transaction fee is 2.2% of the value (including interchange and processing). Across all card payments, check clearings, and ACH transfers, the card network incurs the highest fee-to-value ratio, while ACH represents the lowest. Average B2B card fees exceed retail credit card interchange due to enhanced data processing requirements.
